recognition_excerpt: The City recognizes the Association as the sole and exclusive bargaining agent for all Fire Fighters pursuant to Local Government Code Section 174.101. Recognition of the Association as the exclusive bargaining agent does not make the Association a necessary party to disciplinary agreements between an individual Fire Fighter and the Fire Chief.
classification_excerpt: Article 9, WAGES & BENEFITS, sets the pay scale and Base Wage Increases by Fiscal Year (e.g., 'For Fiscal Year 2023-2024. Effective with the biweekly pay period that begins September 24, 2023, the pay scale attached hereto as Appendix A-...') -- a single firefighter pay scale, not a multi-department classification list.
departments_or_units_covered: Austin Fire Department Fire Fighters only (single occupation, not a bundled multi-department unit)
departments_excerpt: Preamble Section 1: 'This Agreement is made between the City of Austin, Texas...and the Austin Firefighters Association, Local 975 of the International Association of Fire Fighters, AFL-CIO-CLC.'
possible_prior_mechanism_group_overlap: none -- single-occupation safety unit, not comparable to the Houston HOPE / Columbus AFSCME 1632 / Cleveland AFSCME Local 100 bundled non-safety precedent
unresolved_caveat: Exact effective start date (Sept 8, 2023 per the official web page label) is not independently visible inside the extracted text; cycle_start recorded as 2023-09-24, the earliest textually-confirmed wage-effective date, pending a closer read of the signature/ratification page if this matters for later analysis.
recommended_metadata_note: occupation_class=fire is final, not provisional -- no recognition-clause-first ambiguity applies to this row.
